subject: Statistics
title: Regression Discontinuity
description: A fast breakdown of Regression Discontinuity for builders.

hook.text: Students who score 70 or above get a scholarship. Does the scholarship improve graduation rates?

buildup.text: Compare students who scored 71 — just above the cutoff — to those who scored 69 — just below.

discovery.text: Near the cutoff, students are nearly identical except for the scholarship. The jump at 70 is the effect.

twist.text: It only estimates the effect right at the cutoff. The scholarship may help 70-scorers differently than 90-scorers.

climax.text: Regression discontinuity is one of the most credible quasi-experimental designs. The local estimate is very trustworthy.

punchline.text: At the threshold, assignment is as good as random. The jump tells the story.

quiz.question: Why is regression discontinuity considered highly credible?
options: Near the cutoff, subjects are nearly identical — assignment is effectively random
It uses the largest possible sample sizes
It doesn't require any data analysis
